steps.title: Graph the absolute-value equation
steps.content:
Graph the two sides of the equation:
$$y=|-5x+13|$$
$$y=73$$
The intersection points give the two solutions to $|-5x+13|=73$.

steps.title: Read the two x-values
steps.content: The graphs intersect at $x=-12$ and $x=\frac{86}{5}$. Those are the only two solutions because an absolute-value graph can cross a horizontal line at most twice.

steps.title: Add the solutions
steps.content:
The question asks for the sum, not either individual solution:
$$-12+\frac{86}{5}=\frac{-60+86}{5}=\frac{26}{5}$$
This matches, so D is correct.
